key_insight: A pretrained foundation-model interatomic potential, when adaptively fine-tuned during evolutionary crystal structure search, can make crystal structure prediction much more efficient and still accurate.
engineering_relevance: This workflow reduces the DFT labeling burden in multicomponent materials discovery, enabling practical searches for stable phases under conditions such as high pressure.
method: Start from a pretrained MLIP to guide evolutionary CSP exploration.
Iteratively select compact, representative, physically relevant structures for DFT labeling.
Fine-tune the model self-consistently to improve both search efficiency and system-specific accuracy.
evidence_signal: In the Ca-Fe-Ni system, the workflow reproduced the known low-pressure convex hull and predicted a new compound, Ca6FeNi, stable above 100 GPa.
watch_item: Performance still depends on the quality of the pretrained potential and the representativeness of selected DFT samples, so poor transfer or sampling bias could limit reliability.
paper_title: Adaptive fine-tuning of foundation models for crystal structure prediction: Discovery of high-pressure phases in the CaFeNi system
